findings.summary: Residual falsy checks on optional tag IDs inconsistent with explicit `is None` pattern used elsewhere
findings.detail: tags.py uses bare falsy checks (`if processing_tag_id:`, `if optional_tag:`, `if settings.ERROR_TAG_ID:`) while document_iter.py line 63 and the worker modules (ocr/worker.py:68, classifier/worker.py:89) use the correct `is not None` pattern. Since these are all `int | None` types, the falsy check would silently skip tag ID 0. The config enforces positive ints so this is not a runtime bug today, but the inconsistency undermines the improvement that was explicitly made in document_iter.py and the worker modules.
findings.suggestion: Replace the four falsy tag-ID checks in tags.py (lines 63, 129, 137, 159) and stale_lock.py (line 20) with explicit `is not None` checks to match the pattern established in document_iter.py and the workers.

findings.summary: post_tag_id check uses falsy pattern while processing_tag_id on line 63 uses `is not None`
findings.detail: Line 51 uses `if post_tag_id and post_tag_id in tags` (falsy check) while line 63 uses `if processing_tag_id is not None and processing_tag_id in tags` (explicit None check). Both variables are `int | None`. This inconsistency exists within the same function.
findings.suggestion: Change line 51 to `if post_tag_id is not None and post_tag_id in tags` for consistency.

findings.summary: CLASSIFY_PERSON_FIELD_ID uses falsy check instead of `is not None`
findings.detail: Line 284: `if self.settings.CLASSIFY_PERSON_FIELD_ID and result.person` uses a falsy check on an `int | None` field. While the config layer ensures None for non-set values, this is inconsistent with the explicit `is not None` pattern applied to other optional int settings in the same codebase.
findings.suggestion: Change to `if self.settings.CLASSIFY_PERSON_FIELD_ID is not None and result.person`.
